features.title: Handoff ritual — two ownership modes + public checklist
features.problem: The portal's FAQ promises that the client owns the code, accounts, and domain at the end of any engagement ("À toi. Le repo Git, les comptes, le domaine — tout."). That promise is the load-bearing positioning move of the practice — it inverts the typical agency lock-in model and self-selects clients who value autonomy…

features.title: Custom Domain Onboarding — automate Cloudflare Pages domain attach + SSL polling
features.problem: Each buyer wants their app at their own domain (e.g. roger-voice-truck.com). Cloudflare Pages supports unlimited custom domains per project via the HTTP API. To keep onboarding under 5 minutes of operator time, this feature wraps the CF API in: (a) a one-call domain-attach when a tenant is provisioned, (b) generated C…

features.title: Engagement Runtime — Magic-Link Sessions, Persistence & Marc/Visitor Interaction
features.problem: The portal is fully static. A visitor who fills the intake form and leaves loses everything; if they return, they restart from zero. Marc cannot reply on the same surface; he has no inbox of in-flight engagements; there is no asynchronous message thread between the two parties. The engagement-canvas page renders featu…

features.title: Fleet Foundation — multi-tenant primitive for hosting packaged apps
features.problem: Marc sells finished products (SND, soon Volunteer Roster, …) to buyers who each want their own branded app at their own domain (e.g. roger-voice-truck.com) without ever seeing Marc's infra. Today the portal is single-tenant: one D1, one Pages deployment, one set of routes, all bound to Marc. To turn the portal into a…

features.title: Platform Spine — extract shared template primitives after Phase 2
features.problem: Two templates (SND + Volunteer Roster) shipped on top of feat-fleet-foundation in Phase 1 + 2. Now we can observe what actually duplicated between them and extract the shared spine deliberately, instead of guessing. The duplications worth extracting: (1) the auth/tenant gate boilerplate at the top of every API handler…

features.title: Volunteer Roster — second sellable template (Phase 2 abstraction stress-test)
features.problem: App #2 of the package shop. Built deliberately on a different domain shape than SND (no voice, no invoicing, no append-only event log) so the platform spine (tenants, theming, auth, admin shell, custom domains) gets pressure-tested against a second use case before we extract it. Volunteer Roster targets community orga…

features.title: Operator Console — fleet view, new-tenant wizard, audit log at /admin
features.problem: Marc operates a fleet of buyer instances and needs one page that shows it all green at a glance: every tenant's domain, template + version, last activity, billing state, uptime, SSL status. Plus a "Provision new buyer" wizard that turns the conversation "I'd like to buy SND" into a live tenant in under 5 minutes (form…

features.title: Runtime Theme Editor — buyer-controlled appearance with live preview
features.problem: Buyers want visual control over their app so it feels like theirs, not Marc's. The Bonjour retheme already routes every visual decision through CSS custom properties (--accent, --paper, --display, --radius, etc.), so per-tenant theming is just persisting a JSON dictionary of token overrides and injecting them into the…

features.title: Buyer Admin — per-tenant settings hub at /admin on the buyer's domain
features.problem: Each tenant's owner needs a self-serve admin surface to manage their own app: invite team members (their installers, their accountant), view their billing state, export their data, and — via feat-runtime-theme-editor — control the app's appearance. Lives at /admin on the buyer's own domain (e.g. roger-voice-truck.com/…

features.title: Portal App Shell (composition)
features.problem: Layout, navigation, theming, language switching, and global state composing the 7 portal primitives into Marc's specific portal experience. Marc-specific — this is the only feature in the project that is NOT a reusable primitive pattern. Bilingual (French primary, English secondary per Quebec context). Static-first wh…

features.title: Async Status Canvas (primitive)
features.problem: Clients of conventional consultants get sporadic email updates and scheduled milestone meetings, with the actual work-in-progress hidden between checkpoints. The async-status-canvas makes the work continuously visible inside the portal: status flags show stage (triage, planning, building, ready-for-review, shipped), a…

features.title: Case Study Renderer (primitive)
features.problem: Every shipped engagement is potential SEO + portfolio compounding, but only if it is published as a public page in the client's natural language. Most consultants treat case studies as marketing fluff bolted on after delivery. This primitive treats them as primary acquisition infrastructure: takes a structured engagem…

features.title: Self-Service Redirect (Tier 0 Off-Ramp, primitive)
features.problem: Tier 0 clients have real human problems but no commercial budget (civic coordination, household logistics, hobby projects). Most consultants either (a) take the small job and lose money, or (b) reject and burn goodwill. The self-service-redirect invents a third move: a free template/showcase library that says "your pr…

features.title: Structured Intake Form (primitive)
features.problem: Most consulting engagements start with a 60-minute discovery call where the consultant interviews the client live. That model demands real-time scheduling, consumes hours per lead, and excludes side-practice operators with day jobs and families. The structured intake form replaces the discovery call with a guided form…

features.title: Tiered Public Pricing with Showcase Anchoring (primitive)
features.problem: "Cheap" without numbers is rhetoric — every visitor self-imagines a different price. The tiered-public-pricing primitive shows actual prices per tier (Tier 0 self-service / Tier 1 ~$300 / Tier 2 ~$1500 / Tier 3 ~$3000+) with each tier linked to actual past showcase examples that demonstrate what that tier looks like i…

features.title: Triage Queue with Hard Capacity Cap (primitive)
features.problem: Without a hard concurrency cap, a side-practice consultant accepts every triage that's "doable" and ends up with 3+ concurrent active clients eating evenings and weekends. Family-first collapses; the bedrock value falsifies. The triage-queue makes the 1-active-build + 1-in-triage rule structural rather than aspiration…

features.title: Vibe Filter — "We Do / We Don't" Self-Qualify (primitive)
features.problem: Most "ideal client" filters are demographic (industry, size, budget). The vibe-filter is temperamental — it filters for cultural fit, not budget fit. Public "we do / we don't" list lets visitors self-qualify before filling intake. Saves both parties the goodwill cost of triage rejection. People who read the list and t…
